mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at 5% level).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns during early-cycle phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation and elevated unemployment creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high inflation and high unemployment fails to predict negative returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3.0% is followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained labor market tightness and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns across all macro regimes (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers immediate market repricing for aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, negating the tightening fear channel.
